text: **Beat 6 — What this isn't (yet).** Its own honest heading. Cross-person agents: gated behind a trust model we haven't built. Collision detection: a hypothesis, explicitly not the product's identity. The agent-society sketches: illustrative horizon. This section is a *feature* for this audience — it's the anti-hype signature move, and its existence is what licenses the confidence everywhere else. [...] 2. **Identity is the anchor, "clobbering" is only the hook.** Your own doctrine distrusts collision/coordination as narrow and unproven, so I lead the *felt* pain in the subhead but stake the durable-identity moat as beat two — and put "collision detection" in the honest *what-this-isn't* section.
